rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. SEAVIEW INSURANCE CO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Seaview Insurance Companys Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment And Vacate Bail Bond Forfeiture And Exonerate Bond Pursuant To Penal Code 1305(A)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 5, 2026, line 1, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST SEAVIEW INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment And Vacate Bail Bond Forfeiture And Exonerate Bond Pursuant To Penal Code 1305(A) Petitioner Seaview Insurance Company seeks an order setting aside summary judgment, vacating bail bond forfeiture, and exonerating a bond. The court grants Seaview's request for judicial notice and also takes judicial notice of Exhibit A to the People's opposition. The motion is granted, summary judgment is vacated, the forfeiture is set aside, and the bond is exonerated. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: RSF SOCIAL INVESTMENT FUND, INC. VS. DWAYNE REDMON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 5, 2026, line 3, PLAINTIFF RSF SOCIAL INVESTMENT FUND, INC.'S DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT The demurrer to the first amended answer is overruled. RSF Social Investment Fund Inc. raises two issues on demurrer: the denials do not address all facts recited in the verified first amended complaint; and the denials are evasive. There are only three statutory grounds for demurrer to an answer: "(a) The answer does not state facts sufficient to constitute a defense. (b) The answer is uncertain. As used in this subdivision, "uncertain" includes ambiguous and unintelligible. (c) Where the answer pleads a contract, it cannot be ascertained from the answer whether the contract is written or oral." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 430.20.) RSF's objections do not fall within these categories. The answer is sufficiently clear in its denials to survive demurrer. Discovery is a better tool to ascertain a party's position about the allegations in the complaint than pleadings motions; RSF offers no authority that the court can or should analyze the answer line by line for complete responsiveness. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)
